project.name: accidental-synth
project.description: Accidental Synthesizer (AccSyn) is a standalone, native-UI, four-oscillator mono synthesizer for macOS written in Rust, featuring a Slint UI, CoreAudio output, MIDI input, audio effects, and a preset system.

nodes.name: .gitlab-ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitLab CI pipeline configuration running SAST, Semgrep static analysis, and SonarCloud code quality scanning across four stages (test, security, semgrep, sonar).

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Project changelog tracking all releases from the initial 0.1.0 release through CalVer versions, documenting added features, bug fixes, and architectural changes including the multi-crate restructuring.

nodes.name: MIDI_IMPLEMENTATION_CHART.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive MIDI 1.0 implementation chart listing all 128 CC numbers, supported message types, and AccSyn-specific CC mappings for oscillator, filter, envelope, and LFO parameters.

nodes.name: PATCHES.md
nodes.summary: Full reference documentation for the JSON patch file format, describing all top-level keys (oscillators, filter, envelopes, LFOs, mixer, keyboard, effects) with field types, ranges, and examples.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and quick-start guide for Accidental Synthesizer, covering features, build/run instructions, MIDI/audio usage, and links to documentation.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo configuration enabling native CPU target features (target-cpu=native) for AArch64 builds to maximize Apple Silicon performance.
nodes.languageNotes: Single rustflags entry applying -C target-cpu=native exclusively to aarch64 targets, enabling Apple Silicon-specific SIMD and instruction set extensions.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Workspace Cargo manifest defining five member crates, shared dependency versions, and per-crate release profile overrides — accsyn-engine and accsyn-audio at opt-level 3, accidental-synth at opt-level 1 due to Slint macro compatibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses per-crate profile overrides to balance Slint macro expansion (opt-level 1) against DSP hot-path performance (opt-level 3); uses Cargo workspace resolver v3.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the accsyn-midi crate, declaring dependencies on accsyn-core, anyhow, crossbeam-channel, log, midir, and thiserror — all resolved from the workspace.

nodes.name: constants.rs
nodes.summary: Defines all named constants for the MIDI crate: byte indexes into MIDI messages, bitmasks for status byte parsing, channel/connection names, buffer sizes, and polling intervals.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains one non-trivial import (midir::Ignore) used to define the MESSAGE_TYPE_IGNORE_LIST constant, making this the only file with an external dependency declaration.

nodes.name: control_change.rs
nodes.summary: Maps raw MIDI CC numbers (0-127) to typed CC enum variants from accsyn-core, providing the translation layer between incoming MIDI bytes and the synthesizer's parameter system.

nodes.name: get_supported_cc_from_cc_number
nodes.summary: Exhaustive match on MIDI CC number that returns the corresponding typed CC enum variant wrapping the CC value, or None for unmapped CC numbers.

nodes.name: device_monitor.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the DeviceMonitor struct that polls for MIDI input device list changes on a background thread and sends update events over a crossbeam channel for hot-swap support.

nodes.name: DeviceMonitor
nodes.summary: Polls midir for MIDI port list changes every 2 seconds on a dedicated background thread and forwards port list and current-port update events to the control listener via crossbeam.

nodes.name: input_listener.rs
nodes.summary: Handles raw MIDI message parsing and routing: creates midir input connections, demultiplexes MIDI status bytes into typed MidiEvent variants (Note On/Off, CC, pitch bend, program change, channel pressure), and enforces per-channel filtering.

nodes.name: create_midi_input_listener
nodes.summary: Creates a live midir MIDI input connection on the specified port, installing a callback closure that dispatches incoming messages through process_midi_message.

nodes.name: process_midi_message
nodes.summary: Entry point for every incoming MIDI message byte slice: guards against empty messages, filters by channel, converts status bytes to typed events, and sends them to the synthesizer via crossbeam.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Public crate root for accsyn-midi: defines the Midi manager struct that owns MIDI channels, starts the device monitor, creates the virtual CoreMIDI input port, and runs the control listener thread that routes UI and device events.

nodes.name: Midi
nodes.summary: Top-level MIDI subsystem manager that owns crossbeam message channels, Arc-shared note/channel state, and the midir input connection handles; exposes run() to start all MIDI threads and get_midi_message_receiver() for the synthesizer to consume events.

nodes.name: a-slightly-warmer-fm.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining an FM-based warm pad tone with 3-pole filter, specific oscillator levels and tunings, and embedded effect chain settings.

nodes.name: acid-squelch.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset capturing an acid bass squelch timbre with filter envelope modulation and oscillator parameters tuned for resonant TB-303 style sounds.

nodes.name: acid-time.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset for an acid-style sequence timbre, capturing filter sweep and portamento settings typical of acid bass lines.

nodes.name: alien-invasion.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset producing an sci-fi/alien sound using multiple oscillators with detuning, LFO modulation, and effects processing.

nodes.name: ambient-drone.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset for a slow-evolving ambient drone with long attack/release envelopes, subtle LFO modulation, and filter settings for a pad-like quality.

nodes.name: arpable-dirty-fm.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset combining FM synthesis with waveshaping effects to produce a gritty, arpeggiation-friendly lead or bass timbre.

nodes.name: bright-lead.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a bright lead sound with oscillator, filter, envelope, LFO, mixer, and effects parameters embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: buzz-brass.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a buzzy brass-style sound with four oscillators, filter, envelope, LFO, mixer, and effects parameters embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: deep-bass.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a deep bass sound with low-frequency oscillator configuration, filter, envelopes, LFOs, mixer, and effects parameters embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: dirty-bass-echo.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a dirty bass with echo/delay effect, combining distortion-style oscillator settings with an active delay effect chain embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: drifting-pad.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a slowly evolving pad sound with long envelopes, LFO modulation for drift, and layered oscillator configuration embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: electric-piano.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining an electric piano timbre with FM-style oscillator parameters, fast attack, and velocity-sensitive envelope configuration embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: fm-bells.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining an FM-inspired bell sound using oscillator detuning and shape parameters, with characteristic inharmonic tonal content, embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: guys-I-think-I-Broke-It.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset with an experimental or intentionally chaotic sound design, likely featuring extreme parameter values or unusual combinations, embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: house-hits.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a house-style chord hit or stab sound with short envelopes and percussive oscillator configuration embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: init.json
nodes.summary: Factory initialization preset providing a blank-slate default state for the synthesizer with all effects disabled, default envelope and filter values, and four basic oscillators — used as the starting point for new sound design.
nodes.languageNotes: All effects have is_enabled: false. This is the canonical default/init patch analogous to an 'init' preset in traditional DAW instruments.

nodes.name: kick-long.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset emulating a long kick drum using pitch envelope and amplitude shaping to produce a punchy low-frequency percussion sound embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: kick-short.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset emulating a short kick drum with a tight amplitude envelope and pitch sweep, producing a snappy percussive sound embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: plucky-bass.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a plucky bass sound with fast decay envelope and low oscillator configuration, suitable for rhythmic bass lines embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: plucky-keys.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a plucked keyboard-style sound with fast attack, medium decay, and key-tracking filter configuration embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: reverse-bass-swells.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset creating reversed-style bass swells using inverted envelope shapes and slow LFO modulation to produce a rising or swelling bass texture embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: reverse-pulse-lead.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset creating a reverse-style pulsing lead sound with inverted envelopes and pulse-width oscillator parameters embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: sci-fi.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset producing a science-fiction-style texture using unusual oscillator detuning, modulation, and effects combinations to create a futuristic sound embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: shred-auto-arp.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset designed for fast arpeggiated playing with tight envelope settings and potentially LFO-driven pitch modulation to produce a shredding arpeggiator-style sound embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: singing-bowls.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset emulating the resonant, long-sustain tone of singing bowls using slow-attack envelopes, gentle LFO modulation, and harmonic oscillator tuning embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: slide-bass.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset defining a slide or glide bass sound with portamento enabled on oscillators, producing smooth pitch transitions between notes embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: supersaw-5ths-repeater.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset creating a supersaw-style sound with oscillators tuned in fifths for a wide, repeating harmonic texture, producing a rich layered lead or pad sound embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: supersaw-swirl.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset combining supersaw oscillators with LFO-driven swirl modulation for a rich, animated lead or pad texture embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: triangles-and-claves.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset emulating triangle-wave and clave percussion timbres using triangle oscillator shapes and short percussive envelopes embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: wandering-saws.json
nodes.summary: Factory synthesizer preset using detuned sawtooth oscillators with LFO modulation to produce a slowly evolving, wandering textural sound embedded at compile time via include_str!.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the accsyn-core shared-types crate, declaring dependencies on strum, serde, and strum_macros for enum utilities and serialization.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for accsyn-core that publicly re-exports all shared vocabulary modules: event enums, parameter types, default constants, and math helpers.

nodes.name: audio_events.rs
nodes.summary: Defines OutputStreamParameters (lock-free atomic struct for sample rate, buffer size, and channel count) and AudioDeviceUpdateEvents enum for audio device configuration changes.
nodes.languageNotes: OutputStreamParameters uses Arc<AtomicU32> fields for sharing across threads without Mutex — the hot-path pattern used throughout this crate.

nodes.name: OutputStreamParameters
nodes.summary: Thread-safe audio output stream configuration holding sample rate, buffer size, and channel count as Arc-wrapped atomics, with index lookup helpers.

nodes.name: casting.rs
nodes.summary: Provides safe, clippy-suppressed numeric casting helpers (f32/f64/i32 to u8/u32/usize) that clamp values before truncation to prevent undefined behaviour.

nodes.name: f32_to_u8_clamped
nodes.summary: Converts f32 to u8 by clamping to [0, 255] before truncating; used for small index or count values.

nodes.name: f32_to_u32_clamped
nodes.summary: Converts f32 to u32 by clamping to [0, u32::MAX]; accounts for f32 precision rounding of u32::MAX.

nodes.name: f32_to_usize_clamped
nodes.summary: Converts f32 to usize by clamping; intended for delay samples and audio buffer offsets derived from float math.

nodes.name: f64_to_u32_clamped
nodes.summary: Converts f64 to u32 by clamping; used for sample rate values from CoreAudio which are f64.

nodes.name: i32_to_u8_clamped
nodes.summary: Converts i32 to u8 by clamping; used for wave shape indices and small UI-sourced integers.

nodes.name: defaults.rs
nodes.summary: Centralises all synthesizer default values and constants including audio device settings, mixer levels, pitch/portamento ranges, MIDI note frequency table, and per-effect default parameter arrays.
nodes.languageNotes: The 128-entry MIDI_NOTE_FREQUENCIES const array is a compile-time lookup table stored directly in the binary.

nodes.name: effects.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the AudioEffect trait, EffectIndex enum (10 effect types with strum derive), and EffectParameters struct with a factory method that constructs per-effect default parameter sets.

nodes.name: math.rs
nodes.summary: DSP math utility library providing inline functions for dBFS conversion, f32-AtomicU32 bit-casting, frequency-from-cents calculation, MIDI normalization, linear-to-exponential scale mapping, and envelope time curves with full round-trip test coverage.
nodes.languageNotes: All exported functions carry #[inline] for cross-crate inlining from hot-path callers in accsyn-engine; the test module exercises every function including edge cases for NaN, infinity, and epsilon boundaries.

nodes.name: exponential_curve_envelope_time_from_normal_value
nodes.summary: Converts a normalized 0–1 value to an envelope time in milliseconds using a two-segment quadratic curve parameterized by crossover values.

nodes.name: normal_value_from_exponential_curve_envelope_time
nodes.summary: Inverse of exponential_curve_envelope_time_from_normal_value; converts an envelope time in milliseconds back to a normalized 0–1 value for UI round-tripping.

nodes.name: store_f32_as_atomic_u32
nodes.summary: Stores an f32 value into an AtomicU32 via bit-cast representation; core primitive for lock-free f32 parameter sharing on the hot path.

nodes.name: load_f32_from_atomic_u32
nodes.summary: Loads an f32 value from an AtomicU32 via bit-cast representation; paired with store_f32_as_atomic_u32 for lock-free parameter reads on the hot path.

nodes.name: map_value_from_linear_to_exponential_scale
nodes.summary: Maps a linear input to an exponential output scale across configurable input and output ranges, with guards for degenerate cases like zero-length ranges and reversed bounds.

nodes.name: dbfs_to_f32_sample
nodes.summary: Converts a dBFS value to a linear f32 amplitude, returning 0.0 for silence-level or non-finite inputs.

nodes.name: f32s_are_equal
nodes.summary: Compares two f32 values for approximate equality within epsilon tolerance, with special handling for NaN and infinity; the standard comparison primitive used in all math tests.

nodes.name: midi_events.rs
nodes.summary: Defines MidiEvent enum (NoteOn, NoteOff, CC, PitchBend, etc.), CC enum with 80+ control change variants mapped to synthesizer parameters, MidiDeviceUpdateEvents for port management, and MidiChannelIndex.

nodes.name: parameter_types.rs
nodes.summary: Provides typed atomic wrapper structs (NormalizedValue, Hertz, LfoRange, Milliseconds, Cents, Semitones, PitchBend, FilterPoles, Balance, PortamentoBuffers) each with Serialize/Deserialize and Relaxed-ordering AtomicXxx backing for lock-free parameter sharing.
nodes.languageNotes: Every wrapper implements Serialize/Deserialize for preset persistence; float types use AtomicU32 bit-casting while integer types use their native atomic integer variant.

nodes.name: NormalizedValue
nodes.summary: Thread-safe 0.0–1.0 float parameter backed by AtomicU32 bit-cast; implements Serialize/Deserialize for preset save/restore.

nodes.name: Hertz
nodes.summary: Thread-safe frequency parameter in Hz backed by AtomicU32 bit-cast; implements Serialize/Deserialize.

nodes.name: Balance
nodes.summary: Thread-safe stereo balance parameter (-1.0 to 1.0) backed by AtomicU32 bit-cast; implements Serialize/Deserialize.

nodes.name: synth_events.rs
nodes.summary: Defines SynthesizerUpdateEvents enum (parameter change commands from UI/MIDI to the engine) and index enums OscillatorIndex, LFOIndex, and EnvelopeIndex with strum derive for iteration and from_repr conversion.

nodes.name: ui_events.rs
nodes.summary: Defines UIUpdates enum (50+ variants for pushing parameter state from engine/audio/MIDI to the Slint UI) and EnvelopeStage enum for ADSR phase identification.
nodes.languageNotes: UIUpdates::Patches(i32) is a special variant that triggers a full UI refresh; all other variants update only their specific control.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the accsyn-audio crate, declaring dependencies on coreaudio-rs, coreaudio-sys, crossbeam-channel, rtrb, anyhow, log, thiserror, and the internal accsyn-core crate.

nodes.name: constants.rs
nodes.summary: Internal constants for the accsyn-audio crate: channel buffer capacities, stereo buffer counts, channel offset, device list refresh delay, CoreAudio OSStatus success value, and dropout logger interval.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Public API for the accsyn-audio crate: defines the Audio struct (manages device selection, stream configuration, and sample delivery), OutputDevice, OutputChannels, and AudioError, and orchestrates the CoreAudio device monitor, buffer dropout logger, and control listener threads.

nodes.name: Audio
nodes.summary: Main audio output manager: initializes channels, creates the CoreAudio device monitor, starts the buffer dropout logger, and launches the control listener event loop on construction and run().

nodes.name: control_listener.rs
nodes.summary: Spawns the audio device event listener thread that processes AudioDeviceUpdateEvents (device selection, channel assignment, sample rate/buffer size changes) and restarts the audio output loop on device changes.

nodes.name: start_control_listener
nodes.summary: Spawns a background thread that receives AudioDeviceUpdateEvents and handles device selection, channel/sample-rate/buffer-size changes by restarting the audio output loop as needed.

nodes.name: device_monitor.rs
nodes.summary: Implements DeviceMonitor which registers a CoreAudio property listener callback (unsafe FFI) to detect hardware device list changes and forwards OutputDeviceListChanged events via crossbeam channel, plus get_audio_device_list() for enumerating output devices.
nodes.languageNotes: The only unsafe site in the project outside device_monitor.rs is documented: raw pointer FFI required by the CoreAudio AudioObjectAddPropertyListener C API.

nodes.name: DeviceMonitor
nodes.summary: CoreAudio device monitor that registers/removes an AudioObjectPropertyListener and forwards device-list-changed events; implements Drop to deregister the listener on teardown.

nodes.name: get_audio_device_list
nodes.summary: Enumerates CoreAudio output-scope device IDs and returns their display names, filtering to devices that actually support output.

nodes.name: output_loop.rs
nodes.summary: Configures and starts the CoreAudio render callback loop: negotiates physical stream format, sets sample rate and buffer size, installs a generic render callback for f32/i32/i16/i8 sample formats, and initialises the AudioUnit.
nodes.languageNotes: make_render_callback is generic over SampleConvert and requires an explicit data::Interleaved<T>: data::Data where-clause; the render closure reads from an rtrb Consumer ring buffer at realtime priority.

nodes.name: start_main_audio_output_loop
nodes.summary: Configures the CoreAudio AudioUnit for the given output device and stream parameters, installs the appropriate sample-format render callback, and starts the unit; this is the audio hot-path entry point.

nodes.name: sample_convert.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the SampleConvert trait and implements it for f32 (pass-through), i32 (24-bit high-aligned), i16, and i8 with #[inline(always)] conversion functions used inside the realtime render callback.
nodes.languageNotes: #[inline(always)] is used throughout — stronger than #[inline] — because this code executes inside the audio render callback where even a single function-call overhead is unacceptable.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the main accidental-synth binary crate, wiring all workspace sub-crates (accsyn-core, accsyn-engine, accsyn-audio, accsyn-midi) together with Slint, clap, crossbeam-channel, and vergen for version stamping.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo build script that compiles the Slint UI definition (ui/main.slint) with the fluent style and embeds build-date metadata via vergen.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Binary entry point that initializes all synthesizer subsystems (Audio, Synthesizer, MIDI, UI) and wires their communication channels before entering the Slint event loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses clap for CLI argument parsing and env_logger for log initialization; subsystem start order is load-bearing (audio → synthesizer → MIDI → UI → run loop).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates startup by constructing and running Audio, Synthesizer, MIDI, and UI modules, then starting the Slint event loop.

nodes.name: ui.rs
nodes.summary: Core UI module that owns the UI struct (wrapping a crossbeam channel pair), converts engine parameter types into Slint-compatible models, and coordinates callback registration and the UI update listener.
nodes.languageNotes: All Slint set calls happen inside upgrade_in_event_loop closures to guarantee UI thread safety. Conversion helpers (slint_*_from_ui_*) bridge the Rust intermediate structs to Slint-generated types.

nodes.name: UI
nodes.summary: Owns the UI update channel pair and ParameterValues state, exposes get_ui_update_sender() for cross-thread event delivery, and runs callback registration plus the update listener thread.

nodes.name: push_values_to_ui
nodes.summary: Schedules a full UI repaint by marshalling all ParameterValues into Slint model types and calling every set_* setter in the event loop.

nodes.name: update_ui_values_from_module_parameters
nodes.summary: Converts the engine's Arc<ModuleParameters> into a ParameterValues snapshot, applying inverse normalization curves for display.

nodes.name: callbacks.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports sub-modules and provides register_callbacks(), which wires every Slint UI callback to its corresponding engine/MIDI/audio sender.

nodes.name: register_callbacks
nodes.summary: Registers all Slint UI event callbacks by delegating to domain-specific sub-modules (settings, oscillators, filter, modulation, global, effects).

nodes.name: effects.rs
nodes.summary: Registers Slint callbacks for audio effect enable/disable and per-parameter value changes, forwarding them as SynthesizerUpdateEvents.

nodes.name: filter.rs
nodes.summary: Registers Slint callbacks for all filter parameter changes (cutoff, resonance, poles, key tracking, envelope amount, LFO amount), forwarding them to the synthesizer.

nodes.name: global.rs
nodes.summary: Registers Slint callbacks for global synthesizer options: portamento, pitch bend, velocity curve, hard/key sync, polarity, sustain, and output/oscillator mixer controls.

nodes.name: modulation.rs
nodes.summary: Registers Slint callbacks for envelope ADSR stages (attack, decay, sustain, release, invert) and LFO parameters (frequency, shape, phase, phase reset).

nodes.name: oscillators.rs
nodes.summary: Registers Slint callbacks for oscillator parameters: wave shape, course/fine tune, clipper boost, parameter1/2, and pitch envelope amount.

nodes.name: settings.rs
nodes.summary: Registers Slint callbacks for MIDI port/channel selection, audio device and channel configuration, sample rate, buffer size, and patch load/save/delete operations.

nodes.name: constants.rs
nodes.summary: Defines UI-layer constants including audio device sentinel values, oscillator fine-tune defaults, MIDI channel names array, phase range, and MIDI screen slot count.

nodes.name: set_slint_values.rs
nodes.summary: Provides granular set_* helper functions that update individual UI sections (MIDI screen, audio device, oscillators, LFOs, envelopes, filter, mixer, effects, patches) by scheduling upgrade_in_event_loop calls.

nodes.name: structs.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the intermediate Rust structs (UIAudioDevice, UIMidiPort, UIOscillator, UIFilterCutoff, UIFilterOptions, UILfo, UIEnvelope, UIMixer, UIGlobalOptions) that bridge engine atomics to Slint types, including from_synth_parameters constructors with inverse normalization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses #[allow(dead_code)] globally; from_synth_parameters methods apply inverse exponential curves to convert engine Hz/ms values back to 0.0-1.0 normal values for UI display.

nodes.name: UIAudioDevice
nodes.summary: Intermediate data model for audio device selection state, including device lists and selected index values for output device, channels, sample rate, and buffer size.

nodes.name: UIEnvelope
nodes.summary: Intermediate data model for ADSR envelope display values, with from_synth_parameters applying inverse exponential time curves to convert millisecond values to UI normal values.

nodes.name: UIGlobalOptions
nodes.summary: Intermediate data model for global synthesizer options (portamento, pitch bend, velocity curve, hard sync, key sync, polarity, sustain), aggregating parameters from keyboard, oscillator, and envelope parameter blocks.

nodes.name: update_listener.rs
nodes.summary: Spawns a background thread that receives UIUpdates from the engine channel and dispatches each variant to the corresponding Slint setter, with special handling for UIUpdates::Patches which triggers a full UI refresh from the patch list.
nodes.languageNotes: UIUpdates::Patches performs a full parameter reload via update_ui_values_from_module_parameters + push_values_to_ui, while all other variants update only their specific control. Uses #[allow(cast_sign_loss)] for Slint-sourced oscillator indices.

nodes.name: start_ui_update_listener
nodes.summary: Spawns a dedicated thread that loops on ui_update_receiver, matching every UIUpdates variant to its Slint update function or full-refresh path (for Patches variant).

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the accsyn-engine crate, declaring workspace-inherited metadata and dependencies including accsyn-core, crossbeam-channel, rtrb, serde/serde_json, strum, rand, and dirs for patch storage.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for accsyn-engine that publicly re-exports the modules and synthesizer sub-crates, enabling external consumers to access all DSP components.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel that declares and documents all DSP sub-modules: amplifier, effects, envelope, filter, lfo, mixer, and oscillator.

nodes.name: amplifier.rs
nodes.summary: Provides amplify_mono and amplify_stereo functions implementing a VCA (voltage-controlled amplifier) that applies manual and CV gain with clamping, used in the sample generation hot path.

nodes.name: amplify_mono
nodes.summary: Applies optional manual and control-voltage gain to a mono sample, clamping all values to valid ranges.

nodes.name: amplify_stereo
nodes.summary: Delegates to amplify_mono for both channels of a stereo sample pair and returns the result as a tuple.

nodes.name: envelope.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the ADSR envelope generator with atomic parameter sharing (EnvelopeParameters), a state machine (Stage enum), and the Envelope DSP struct for sample-accurate gate and level computation.

nodes.name: EnvelopeParameters
nodes.summary: Shared atomic parameter block for an ADSR envelope, providing assign_from() for preset loading and Serialize/Deserialize for JSON persistence.

nodes.name: Envelope
nodes.summary: DSP envelope generator with a stage state machine (Off/Attack/Decay/Sustain/Release), producing per-sample control voltage via generate() and responding to gate events via check_gate().

nodes.name: filter.rs
nodes.summary: Implements a resonant four-pole Moog ladder lowpass filter with atomic parameter sharing (FilterParameters), key tracking, envelope/LFO modulation, and non-linear saturation at the final stage.
nodes.languageNotes: Derived from musicdsp.org algorithm; uses a denormal guard (1e-25) at each stage output to prevent CPU stalls from subnormal floats in the hot path.

nodes.name: FilterParameters
nodes.summary: Shared atomic parameter block for the ladder filter, providing assign_from() for preset loading and Serialize/Deserialize for JSON persistence.

nodes.name: Filter
nodes.summary: DSP resonant ladder filter with independent left/right state (LadderState), coefficient recalculation on parameter change, and a process() method for per-sample stereo filtering.

nodes.name: lfo.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the low-frequency oscillator module with atomic parameter sharing (LfoParameters), center/range scaling, phase control, and wave shape selection backed by the Oscillator DSP.

nodes.name: LfoParameters
nodes.summary: Shared atomic parameter block for an LFO, providing assign_from() for preset loading and Serialize/Deserialize for JSON persistence.

nodes.name: Lfo
nodes.summary: DSP LFO that wraps an Oscillator, scaling output by center_value and range, with configurable frequency, wave shape, phase, and reset support via set_parameters().

nodes.name: mixer.rs
nodes.summary: Provides quad_mix and output_mix functions for combining four oscillator signals with per-strip level/balance/mute into a normalized stereo output.

nodes.name: effects.rs
nodes.summary: Declares AudioEffectParameters (atomic shared state per effect) and the Effects chain that instantiates and sequences all audio effects (saturation, compressor, wavefolder, clipper, gate, rectifier, bit shifter, delay, autopan, tremolo).

nodes.name: AudioEffectParameters
nodes.summary: Shared atomic parameter block for a single audio effect, with assign_from() for preset loading and Serialize/Deserialize for JSON persistence.

nodes.name: Effects
nodes.summary: Audio effects chain that owns all boxed AudioEffect trait objects, applies set_parameters() from shared atomics, and processes stereo samples through each enabled effect in sequence.

nodes.name: main.slint
nodes.summary: Root Slint UI file that declares the top-level AccidentalSynth window component, composing all panel components (header, oscillators, filter, global, effects) into the final application layout and wiring all callbacks to Rust.
nodes.languageNotes: Slint root window — imports all panel .slint files; exposes properties and callbacks that the Rust side binds to via generated code.

nodes.name: theme.slint
nodes.summary: Central theme singleton exporting all design tokens — colours, sizes, fonts, labels, and panel-specific measurements — consumed throughout the entire UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Slint global singleton (`export global Theme`); all properties are out-properties read by other components.

nodes.name: constants.slint
nodes.summary: Exports two Slint globals: PatchState (tracks patch generation counter to force reactive refreshes) and Constant (module indices, tuning limits, and dropdown string arrays shared across panels).
nodes.languageNotes: PatchState.generation is the patch-refresh bus: any panel that needs to reset UI state on preset load watches this property.

nodes.name: components.slint
nodes.summary: Library of reusable synthesizer UI components — labeled sliders, envelopes, LFO sections, dropdowns, toggle switches, and mixer widgets — used across all panel files.
nodes.languageNotes: All slider and dropdown components reactively sync with PatchState.generation to reset displayed values on preset load without breaking the declarative binding model.

nodes.name: custom-widgets.slint
nodes.summary: Implements custom slider widgets (VerticalSlider, HorizontalSlider, BalanceSlider) with a custom visual track-and-handle design, plus the supporting SliderHandle and SliderTrack/BalanceTrack primitives.
nodes.languageNotes: Custom sliders use TouchArea for dragging and implement double-click-to-reset via a stored patch-value property; they do not use Slint's built-in Slider to achieve the custom visual style.

nodes.name: effects-panel.slint
nodes.summary: EffectsPanel component that lays out all ten effect boxes (Saturation, Compressor, Wavefolder, Bitshifter, Clipper, Gate, Rectifier, AutoPan, Tremolo, Delay) in two rows and routes enable and parameter callbacks.

nodes.name: effects.slint
nodes.summary: Defines the EffectsValues data struct and all individual effect UI components (EffectBox base, Saturation, Compressor, Wavefolder, Bitshifter, Clipper, Gate, Rectifier, AutoPan, Tremolo, Delay), each with parameter sliders and enable toggle.
nodes.languageNotes: EffectBox is the shared base component that all specific effects inherit from; each effect watches PatchState.generation to refresh its enabled state on preset load.

nodes.name: filter-panel.slint
nodes.summary: FilterPanel component composing cutoff/resonance sliders, filter options (poles, key tracking, EG/LFO amounts), a filter envelope section, and filter LFO section into the filter tab column.

nodes.name: global-panel.slint
nodes.summary: GlobalPanel component housing the mixer section (output and per-oscillator strips), amp and pitch envelopes, mod-wheel LFO, and oscillator options (portamento, pitch bend, hard sync, key sync, sustain, velocity curve).

nodes.name: header-panel.slint
nodes.summary: HeaderPanel component rendering the logo, patch selector dropdown, MIDI note scroller, version text, and settings gear icon; embeds the SettingsWindow popup and routes all audio/MIDI/patch callbacks.

nodes.name: oscillator-panel.slint
nodes.summary: OscillatorsPanel that instantiates four OscControlsPanel instances (Sub, Osc1, Osc2, Osc3), each controlling wave shape, coarse/fine tune, clipper boost, pitch envelope amount, and wave-shape-dependent parameters.

nodes.name: settings-panel.slint
nodes.summary: Settings popup window with audio device configuration, MIDI port selection, synth options (polarity), and patch save/delete sections; exposes AudioDevice, MidiPort, and PatchStatus structs.

nodes.name: autopan.rs
nodes.summary: Auto-panning DSP effect that drives stereo panning with an internal LFO using equal-power panning (cosine/sine law); implements AudioEffect::process_samples with lazy parameter updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses lazy parameter comparison via f32s_are_equal to avoid redundant LFO reconfiguration on every sample; equal-power pan uses PI/2 scaling.

nodes.name: bitshifter.rs
nodes.summary: Bit-crusher DSP effect that quantizes audio to a reduced bit depth with wet/dry blend; maps a normalized slider value to a bit count and applies ceiling-based quantization preserving sign.

nodes.name: clipper.rs
nodes.summary: Hard clipper DSP effect with pre-gain, threshold, notch mode (zero-crossing above threshold), and post-gain makeup; clamps output to MAX_SAMPLE_VALUE.

nodes.name: compressor.rs
nodes.summary: Simple colour compressor DSP effect applying ratio-based gain reduction above a threshold with makeup gain and wet/dry blend; preserves sign via signum.

nodes.name: constants.rs
nodes.summary: Shared constants for all audio effects modules: threshold limits, bit-depth bounds, compressor ratio/gain ranges, LFO defaults, and delay buffer sizes.

nodes.name: delay.rs
nodes.summary: Echo/delay DSP effect using a power-of-2 ring buffer with linear interpolation between delay taps, exponential smoothing on delay time changes, and feedback; resets buffer when disabled.
nodes.languageNotes: Buffer size is required to be a power of 2 (validated at compile time with a const assert) to allow bitwise wrapping: `(index) & (buffer_len - 1)` instead of modulo.

nodes.name: gate.rs
nodes.summary: Noise gate DSP effect that silences samples whose absolute value (after pre-gain) falls below a threshold, with optional post-gate makeup gain.

nodes.name: rectifier.rs
nodes.summary: Wave rectifier DSP effect supporting half-wave (zeros negatives) and full-wave (folds negatives to positive) modes with wet/dry blend control.

nodes.name: saturation.rs
nodes.summary: Saturation DSP effect with six algorithm modes (analog modeled, tube-like, soft clipping, wave shaping, sine shaper, Chebyshev polynomial), selectable via SaturationMode enum, with post-saturation gain reduction.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses strum's FromRepr to convert a float mode index to the SaturationMode enum; each algorithm applies its own makeup gain to compensate for level differences.

nodes.name: tremolo.rs
nodes.summary: Tremolo DSP effect that amplitude-modulates the signal using an internal LFO with configurable rate, depth, and wave shape; uses lazy parameter comparison to avoid redundant LFO updates.

nodes.name: wavefolder.rs
nodes.summary: Wave folder DSP effect that reflects audio samples that exceed positive or negative thresholds back into range; supports symmetric and asymmetric positive/negative fold amounts.

nodes.name: AutoPan
nodes.summary: Auto-pan effect struct holding an Lfo and cached LfoParameters; implements AudioEffect to produce stereo panning modulation on each sample pair.

nodes.name: BitShifter
nodes.summary: Stateless bit-crusher effect struct implementing AudioEffect; quantizes audio to a reduced bit depth with wet/dry blend on each sample call.

nodes.name: Clipper
nodes.summary: Stateless clipper effect struct implementing AudioEffect; applies threshold clipping with optional notch mode and pre/post gain.

nodes.name: Compressor
nodes.summary: Stateless compressor effect struct implementing AudioEffect; applies ratio compression above threshold with makeup gain and wet/dry blend.

nodes.name: Delay
nodes.summary: Delay effect struct with a ring buffer, write index, and smoothed delay-time state; implements AudioEffect with linear interpolation between taps and buffer reset on disable.

nodes.name: Gate
nodes.summary: Stateless noise gate effect struct implementing AudioEffect; zeros samples below threshold after applying pre-gain, then applies post-gate makeup gain.

nodes.name: Rectifier
nodes.summary: Stateless wave rectifier effect struct implementing AudioEffect; supports half-wave and full-wave rectification with wet/dry blend.

nodes.name: Saturation
nodes.summary: Stateless saturation effect struct implementing AudioEffect; dispatches to one of six saturation algorithm functions based on the mode parameter.

nodes.name: Tremolo
nodes.summary: Tremolo effect struct holding an Lfo and cached LfoParameters; implements AudioEffect to amplitude-modulate samples using the LFO output.

nodes.name: WaveFolder
nodes.summary: Stateless wave folder effect struct implementing AudioEffect; folds stereo samples at configurable positive and negative thresholds, supporting symmetrical and asymmetrical modes.

nodes.name: oscillator.rs
nodes.summary: Core oscillator module dispatcher that manages four oscillator instances, exposes the WaveShape enum and OscillatorParameters struct, and delegates per-sample generation to pluggable waveform implementations via the GenerateWave trait. Handles hard sync, portamento, key sync, pitch bend, clipper boost, and aftertouch.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Arc<AtomicBool> for the hard sync buffer shared between the source and synced oscillators — a deliberate lock-free pattern for the realtime thread. f32 frequency state is stored directly on the Tuning struct (not atomic) because it is only written from the sample generation thread.

nodes.name: Oscillator
nodes.summary: Core oscillator module that generates audio samples using a dynamically dispatched, selectable wave shape. Manages tuning, hard sync, portamento, clipper boost, and key sync.

nodes.name: OscillatorParameters
nodes.summary: Shared atomic parameter block for controlling an oscillator from the UI thread, including wave shape, tuning, key sync, hard sync, portamento, clipper boost, and pitch envelope amount.

nodes.name: generate_wave_trait.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the GenerateWave trait that all waveform implementations must satisfy, providing the interface for per-sample generation, phase control, shape parameters, and wave shape identification.
nodes.languageNotes: This is the central polymorphism boundary for the oscillator system — the Oscillator struct holds a Box<dyn GenerateWave + Send + Sync> allowing zero-cost dispatch after construction.

nodes.name: constants.rs
nodes.summary: Oscillator-wide constants covering phase bounds, waveform defaults, clipper boost range, AM/FM shape parameters, portamento defaults, and MIDI note frequency range limits.

nodes.name: am.rs
nodes.summary: Amplitude modulation oscillator that generates samples by modulating a carrier sine wave with a modulator sine, supporting both ring modulation and AM synthesis modes via shape parameters.

nodes.name: broken.rs
nodes.summary: Experimental 'broken' waveform oscillator producing an unconventional timbral output through shape parameter-controlled distortion, included as a creative sound design option.

nodes.name: fm.rs
nodes.summary: Frequency modulation oscillator implementing two-operator FM synthesis where shape parameters control the modulation depth and ratio, producing complex harmonic spectra.

nodes.name: noise.rs
nodes.summary: White noise generator oscillator producing random samples, useful for percussion and noise-based sound design.

nodes.name: pulse.rs
nodes.summary: Pulse wave oscillator with variable pulse width controlled by shape parameter 1, supporting pulse-width modulation via the modulation input for classic synthesizer sounds.

nodes.name: ramp.rs
nodes.summary: Ramp (reverse sawtooth) wave oscillator using phase accumulation to generate a downward-sloping waveform with rich harmonic content.

nodes.name: saw.rs
nodes.summary: Sawtooth wave oscillator using phase accumulation, producing the classic saw waveform with all harmonics present and strong harmonic content.

nodes.name: sine.rs
nodes.summary: Pure sine wave oscillator using phase accumulation with radians per cycle, implementing GenerateWave with support for frequency modulation via the modulation input.

nodes.name: square.rs
nodes.summary: Square wave oscillator using phase accumulation, generating a +1/-1 waveform with odd harmonics characteristic of classic synthesizer tones.

nodes.name: supersaw.rs
nodes.summary: Multi-voice detuned supersaw oscillator that layers multiple sawtooth waves with slight frequency offsets, producing the wide, dense sound characteristic of trance and EDM leads.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains multiple internal phase accumulators and mixes them — more computationally expensive than single-voice oscillators but avoids allocations in the hot path.

nodes.name: triangle.rs
nodes.summary: Triangle wave oscillator using phase accumulation and absolute-value folding to produce a smooth triangle waveform with only odd harmonics at reduced amplitudes.

nodes.name: constants.rs
nodes.summary: Synthesizer-wide constants covering MIDI parameters, envelope and LFO indices, audio loop sleep duration, patch management limits and status messages, and compile-time embedded factory patch data via include_str!.
nodes.languageNotes: Factory patches are embedded at compile time using include_str! macros — 30 factory presets are baked into the binary. The SYSTEM_PATCHES slice and SYSTEM_PATCH_INIT_PARAMETERS constant expose them at runtime without file I/O.

nodes.name: event_listener.rs
nodes.summary: Spawns the UI update event listener thread that receives SynthesizerUpdateEvents from the UI thread and dispatches them to parameter-setting functions, covering all oscillator, envelope, LFO, filter, effects, mixer, and preset operations.

nodes.name: start_update_event_listener
nodes.summary: Spawns the synthesizer event listener thread that receives all SynthesizerUpdateEvents from the UI and routes them to parameter update functions, handling every synthesizer parameter change event.

nodes.name: midi_messages.rs
nodes.summary: Handles all incoming MIDI messages including Note On/Off, pitch bend, channel pressure (aftertouch), program change, and control change (CC) routing — mapping MIDI values to synthesizer parameter updates and UI feedback.

nodes.name: midi_value_converters.rs
nodes.summary: Pure conversion functions that map normalized 0.0-1.0 MIDI values to synthesizer parameter ranges, including linear, exponential, and discrete-step mappings for filter cutoff, LFO frequency, envelope times, velocity curve, and wave shape index.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the top-level Synthesizer struct that owns all DSP module parameters, coordinates the MIDI event listener thread, update event listener, and sample generation thread. Also defines ModuleParameters, MixerParameters, KeyboardParameters, and QuadMixerInput shared parameter structs.

nodes.name: Synthesizer
nodes.summary: Top-level synthesizer struct that bootstraps MIDI input, UI event handling, and the audio sample generation loop. Owns shared ModuleParameters and the patch collection via Arc references.

nodes.name: ModuleParameters
nodes.summary: Central shared atomic parameter container for all synthesizer modules — filter, mixer, keyboard, LFOs, envelopes, oscillators, and effects — accessed concurrently from the MIDI, UI event, and sample generation threads.

nodes.name: sample_generator.rs
nodes.summary: The realtime audio hot-path — spawns the sample generation thread that continuously reads atomic parameters, runs all DSP modules (oscillators, envelopes, LFOs, filter, effects), and writes interleaved stereo samples into the rtrb ring buffer. No allocations or logging in the inner loop.
nodes.languageNotes: The inner `while local_buffer.len() < stereo_buffer_size` loop is the realtime hot path. Buffer capacity is pre-reserved once; no Vec allocation occurs inside the loop. Debug-only profiling via #[cfg(debug_assertions)] logs budget usage every 500 buffers.

nodes.name: sample_generator
nodes.summary: Public entry point that blocks until a ring buffer producer is received from the audio module, then spawns the sample generation thread.

nodes.name: generate_audio_samples
nodes.summary: The core realtime DSP loop — processes all synthesizer modules per buffer, generates interleaved stereo samples, and writes them to the ring buffer. Must complete within the audio callback budget (~2-10ms).

nodes.name: set_parameters.rs
nodes.summary: Collection of parameter-setting functions that translate normalized UI values into synthesizer atomic parameters — covering oscillator, envelope, LFO, filter, effects, mixer, and preset loading via assign_from().

nodes.name: patches.rs
nodes.summary: Patch and preset management system: loads factory patches from compile-time embedded JSON, discovers and loads user patches from ~/Library/Application Support/AccidentalSynthesizer/patches/, and handles save, delete, and rename operations with path traversal safety checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Factory patches are embedded at compile time in constants.rs via include_str!; this module deserializes them at runtime. User patches live in platform dirs and are discovered at startup. Path traversal is checked explicitly before any file write or delete.

nodes.name: dsp_chain.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test verifying that a gate-on MIDI event produces non-silent audio output through the full oscillator and envelope DSP chain at 48kHz sample rate.

nodes.name: synthesizer.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test verifying that Synthesizer::new() succeeds with default stream parameters, exercising the full construction path including embedded init-patch deserialization and user patch directory discovery.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: The accidental-synth binary crate: Slint window setup, event-driven callbacks for every synthesizer control panel, the engine-to-UI update listener, and the build script that compiles Slint files and embeds version metadata.

layers.name: Synthesis Engine
layers.description: The accsyn-engine crate: the real-time DSP core containing oscillators (11 waveforms including FM, AM, supersaw), ADSR envelopes, resonant filters, LFOs, 10 audio effects, the main sample generation hot-path, MIDI message handling, and the preset load/apply system.

layers.name: Audio Output
layers.description: The accsyn-audio crate: CoreAudio stream setup, the real-time render callback that pulls from the rtrb ring buffer, CoreAudio device hot-swap monitoring via FFI, and multi-format sample conversion (i8 through f32).

layers.name: MIDI Input
layers.description: The accsyn-midi crate: midir-based virtual MIDI port, raw MIDI message parsing and CC routing, and background polling for MIDI device list changes.

layers.name: Core Shared Types
layers.description: The accsyn-core crate: zero-dependency foundation providing shared event enums (AudioEvents, MidiEvents, UIUpdates, SynthesizerUpdateEvents), lock-free atomic parameter structs (Arc<AtomicU32>), f32/AtomicU32 bit-cast helpers, DSP math utilities, and synthesizer parameter defaults.

layers.name: Factory Presets
layers.description: 30 JSON patch files embedded at compile time via include_str!, each defining a complete set of synthesizer parameter values for one factory sound (basses, leads, pads, drums, FM textures, and experimental timbres).

layers.name: Build & CI Configuration
layers.description: Workspace-level Cargo manifest defining the five-crate workspace and per-crate optimization profiles, the .cargo/config.toml enabling native AArch64 CPU features, and the GitLab CI pipeline running SAST, Semgrep, and SonarCloud analysis.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including the README quick-start, full MIDI 1.0 CC implementation chart, JSON patch file format specification, and the release changelog.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README establishes what AccSyn is — a standalone, four-oscillator mono synthesizer for macOS written in Rust — and maps the five-crate workspace that the rest of the tour explores. Reading it first gives you the mental model: UI events flow down to the engine, audio flows out through CoreAudio, and MIDI events flow in from an external device. Every subsequent step you take corresponds to one of the components described here.

tour.title: Workspace Manifest
tour.description: The root Cargo.toml defines the five-crate workspace and sets per-crate optimization levels that are central to how the project achieves real-time safety. `accsyn-engine` and `accsyn-audio` are built at opt-level=3 so their DSP and audio callback hot paths can complete within the ~2–10ms CoreAudio deadline, while `accidental-synth` (the Slint UI binary) is intentionally held at opt-level=1 because aggressive optimization is incompatible with the Slint macro expansion. Understanding these constraints upfront explains many design decisions you will encounter throughout the codebase.
tour.languageLesson: Cargo workspaces let multiple crates share a single Cargo.lock and dependency resolution, while profile overrides (e.g., [profile.release.package.accsyn-engine]) let each crate be compiled at a different optimization level within the same build.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: main.rs is the orchestrator: it initializes logging, creates all the inter-thread channels (MPSC and ring-buffer), spawns the engine thread, audio output thread, and MIDI input thread, then hands control to the Slint UI event loop on the main thread. Everything that happens at runtime starts from the wiring performed here. Reading this file tells you exactly which channels connect which subsystems and which threads own which responsibilities.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's ownership model enforces at compile time that each channel endpoint (sender/receiver) is owned by exactly one thread. When main.rs moves a sender into a spawned thread closure, the borrow checker prevents that sender from being used again on the main thread — making data-race freedom a compile-time guarantee rather than a runtime hope.

tour.title: Core Shared Types
tour.description: accsyn-core is the foundation every other crate builds on. Its lib.rs re-exports the full vocabulary of the system: event enums for MIDI, audio, and UI messages; the atomic parameter structs used for lock-free hot-path sharing; DSP math utilities with mandatory `#[inline]` attributes; and the f32/AtomicU32 bit-cast helpers that allow sharing floating-point parameters across threads without a mutex. Because this crate has zero internal dependencies, it can be imported by all other crates without creating circular dependency issues.
tour.languageLesson: Rust has no AtomicF32 in std. The project stores f32 parameters in AtomicU32 using transmute-based bit-casting (store_f32_in_atomic_u32 / load_f32_from_atomic_u32). This gives lock-free atomic reads in the hot path while keeping the type system happy, at the cost of requiring discipline not to substitute a Mutex<f32> when adding new parameters.

tour.title: Inter-Thread Event Contracts
tour.description: Three event enums define the message contracts that cross thread boundaries: MidiEvents carries NoteOn, NoteOff, CC, and pitch-bend messages from the MIDI thread into the engine; SynthesizerUpdateEvents carries UI control changes (knob moves, preset loads) from the UI thread into the engine; and UIUpdates carries engine state back to the UI thread so controls stay in sync after a preset load. These three files are the formal API surface between AccSyn's subsystems — everything flows through one of these three channels.

tour.title: MIDI Input Pipeline
tour.description: The accsyn-midi crate owns everything on the MIDI side of the wall. lib.rs creates a virtual MIDI port (so AccSyn appears as a device in any DAW without physical hardware), monitors for device changes via device_monitor.rs, and hands incoming bytes to input_listener.rs, which parses raw MIDI bytes into MidiEvents variants and routes them onto the engine channel. control_change.rs maps the 128 MIDI CC numbers to a typed enum, preventing magic-number bugs throughout the codebase. The MIDI_IMPLEMENTATION_CHART.md documents exactly which CC numbers are mapped and what each controls.

tour.title: The Synthesizer Struct
tour.description: synthesizer/mod.rs is the heart of the engine: it owns all DSP module instances (oscillators, filter, envelopes, LFOs, effects chain, mixer, amplifier), manages the monophonic voice state (current note, gate, sustain pedal), and exposes three entry points that main.rs calls — one for handling MIDI events, one for handling UI events, and one for generating the next buffer of samples. It is the single object that the audio thread holds a reference to. The integration tests in tests/synthesizer.rs verify it starts up without panic, and tests/dsp_chain.rs verifies that a NoteOn followed by sample generation actually produces non-zero audio output.

tour.title: The Sample Generation Hot Path
tour.description: sample_generator.rs is the single most performance-critical file in the project. It is called by the audio thread on every buffer fill and must complete within the CoreAudio deadline (~2–10ms) with zero heap allocations and zero logging. The inner loop calls each DSP module in signal-flow order: oscillators produce raw waveform samples, which pass through the filter, then the amplifier envelope, then the effects chain, and finally the stereo mixer before being written to the ring buffer that the audio thread drains. Any regression here — an allocation, a mutex contention, a log call — causes audible dropouts.
tour.languageLesson: Real-time audio code follows the 'real-time safe' constraint: no heap allocation (no Vec::push, Box::new, String formatting), no blocking (no Mutex::lock that could sleep), no system calls, and no logging. Rust helps by making allocations visible in the type system — if you accidentally introduce a Box or String in a hot path, it's usually obvious from the type signature.

tour.title: Oscillators and Waveforms
tour.description: oscillator.rs is the dispatcher that owns four oscillator instances and mixes their outputs. Each oscillator delegates waveform generation to a concrete type that implements the GenerateWave trait defined in generate_wave_trait.rs — this is the extension point for adding new waveforms without touching the dispatcher. The waveform implementations include standard shapes (sine, saw, square, triangle, pulse, ramp, noise) plus synthesizer-specific ones (supersaw for detuned multi-saw density, FM for frequency modulation, AM for amplitude modulation, and 'broken' for intentional glitch artifacts). Each implementation reads parameters from the shared atomics without locking.
tour.languageLesson: The GenerateWave trait is a classic Rust trait-object dispatch point: each waveform type implements the same interface, and the oscillator dispatcher holds a Box<dyn GenerateWave>. Because this is in the hot path, an alternative design using an enum with a match arm per variant could avoid the vtable indirection — a trade-off between extensibility and raw throughput.

tour.title: Filter, Envelope, and Modulation
tour.description: After the oscillators, the signal passes through three interconnected DSP modules. envelope.rs implements a standard ADSR state machine that gate events (NoteOn/NoteOff from the MIDI thread) trigger; amplifier.rs uses one envelope for volume shaping and scales by MIDI velocity. filter.rs implements a resonant lowpass filter with three simultaneous modulation sources: key tracking (cutoff follows the played note), an envelope from a second ADSR, and an LFO from lfo.rs. These files collectively implement the classic subtractive synthesis signal path that defines AccSyn's core sound.

tour.title: Effects Chain
tour.description: effects.rs in the modules layer is the dispatcher that iterates over the ordered effects chain and routes audio through each enabled effect. Each effect is its own module: saturation, compressor, wave folder, clipper, gate, wave rectifier, bit shifter, tape delay, auto-pan, and tremolo. The dispatcher reads each effect's enabled flag and parameters from shared atomics at buffer time — no locking required. Adding a new effect means implementing the processing logic in a new file and registering it in the dispatcher and the Effects enum in accsyn-core.

tour.title: Audio Output Layer
tour.description: accsyn-audio owns the CoreAudio integration. start_main_audio_output_loop() in lib.rs negotiates the output stream with CoreAudio, spawns the device monitor (which uses raw CoreAudio FFI to listen for hardware changes), and registers output_loop.rs as the CoreAudio render callback. The render callback drains samples from the rtrb lock-free ring buffer that the engine thread fills, then converts them from internal f32 to the actual output format (i8, i16, i24, i32, or f32) via sample_convert.rs. The ring buffer is the only shared state between the engine thread and the audio callback — it deliberately avoids any mutex.
tour.languageLesson: rtrb is a single-producer single-consumer lock-free ring buffer. The engine thread (producer) writes samples; the CoreAudio render callback (consumer) reads them. Because only one thread writes and one reads, no atomic compare-and-swap is needed — just atomic index updates with Release/Acquire ordering, which is cheaper than a mutex and safe from the realtime thread.

tour.title: Preset System
tour.description: patches.rs implements a two-tier patch system: factory patches are embedded at compile time via include_str! macros pointing to JSON files in the crates/accsyn-engine/src/synthesizer/patches/ directory, making them part of the binary with no file-system dependency at runtime. User patches are read from and written to ~/Library/Application Support/AccidentalSynthesizer/patches/ via the dirs crate. set_parameters.rs applies a loaded patch to the live synthesizer by calling assign_from() on each parameter struct, which writes all fields from a deserialized plain struct into the live atomics. PATCHES.md documents the JSON schema for authoring patches by hand.
tour.languageLesson: include_str! embeds a file's contents as a &'static str at compile time. For factory patches this means the binary ships with all patches baked in — no installer step, no missing-file error at runtime. The trade-off is that adding a factory patch requires a recompile.

tour.title: UI Bridge: Slint and Rust
tour.description: The UI layer spans two worlds: the Slint markup files define the visual layout and property bindings, while the Rust side in ui.rs and its sub-modules wires those properties to the engine. update_listener.rs runs a background loop receiving UIUpdates from the engine and pushing values into Slint properties via the Slint event loop. The callbacks/ sub-modules (oscillators, filter, modulation, effects, global, settings) each register Slint callback handlers that translate knob moves and button clicks into SynthesizerUpdateEvents sent to the engine. set_slint_values.rs handles the inverse conversions, since Slint's percentage-range values often need mapping to the engine's Hz or dB ranges.
tour.languageLesson: Slint's Rust integration generates a strongly-typed Rust struct from the .slint markup at compile time (via the build script). Properties become getter/setter methods; callbacks become register_* methods. This means UI property names are checked at compile time — a renamed Slint property becomes a Rust compile error, not a silent runtime mismatch.

tour.title: Slint UI Layout and CI
tour.description: main.slint is the root composition file that imports and arranges all panel components — header, oscillator, filter, modulation, effects, global, and settings. The build script (build.rs) compiles all .slint files and embeds the application version string via vergen so the UI always shows the exact build version. The GitLab CI pipeline (.gitlab-ci.yml) runs cargo clippy, cargo fmt --check, and cargo test on every push, enforcing the code quality bar that keeps the hot-path constraints and no-unwrap rules consistent across contributors.
